items.q: How do you say 'I study maths' in French?
items.why: Students drop the article like English. School subjects in French take the DEFINITE article: 'j'aime le français', 'j'étudie les sciences', 'je déteste l'histoire'. This is the rule for general nouns.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Paris' (near future) in French?
items.why: Students add 'à' between aller and the infinitive. Futur proche structure is ALLER (conjugated) + INFINITIVE, with no preposition: 'je vais manger', 'je vais aller', 'je vais partir'.

items.q: How do you say 'I like coffee' in French?
items.why: English drops articles for general likes/dislikes; French keeps them. For general preferences, use the DEFINITE article: j'aime le chocolat, je déteste les épinards.

items.q: Which sentence is correct for 'You must respect the teachers'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Il faut respecter les profs. “Il faut respectez les profs” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does 'Je vais étudier l'histoire à l'université' mean?
items.correct: I am going to study history at university
items.random_wrong: I am studying history at university
items.why: The answer examiners want is: I am going to study history at university. “I studied history at university” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If a French lycéen passes the bac at 18 and does a 3-year licence, how old when they finish?
items.why: Exact answer: 21. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
